title: Short-run production costs: foundational concepts
description: Practice what you have learned about the difference between marginal cost, average variable cost, and fixed costs in this exercise.
topic_path: Production and costs in the short run

assessment_items.question_content:
Melanie made $100$ muffins in her bakery and used the formula below to calculate one of the costs associated with making these muffins:

$\dfrac{\text{Fixed cost of making} 100 \text{muffins} }{100} + \dfrac{\text{Variable cost of making} 100 \text{muffins} }{100}$

**What is Melanie calculating?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Melanie is adding average fixed costs and average variable costs of producing $100$ muffins.
$\text{Average fixed costs} = \dfrac{\text{Fixed costs}}{100}$
$\text{Average variable costs} (100) =\dfrac{\text{Variable cost}(100)}{100}$
Average total cost is the sum of average fixed cost and average variable cost:

$\begin{align}\text{Average total cost}(Q) &= \dfrac{\text{Total cost}}{\text{Quantity}}\\\\
& = \dfrac{\text{(Fixed costs} + \text{Variable Costs)}}{\text{Quantity}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{\text{Fixed costs}}{\text{Quantity}} + \dfrac{\text{Variable cost}}{\text{Quantity}}\\\\
& =AFC + AVC(Q)\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a way to calculate the marginal cost of the $100^{\text{th}}$ unit of output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $\text{Total cost of producing } 100 \text{ units} - \text{Total cost of producing }99\text{ units}$
$\dfrac{\text{Fixed costs of producing } 100\text{ units} + \text{Variable costs of producing } 100\text{ units}}{100}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes average variable cost?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The costs that vary as output changes, per unit of output
The cost of producing one more unit of output
The total costs that vary as output changes, per unit of output
The costs that do not vary as output changes, per unit of output
The costs that do not vary as output changes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes marginal cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cost of producing one more unit of output
The cost of producing a typical unit of output
The costs that vary as output changes, per unit of output
The costs that do not vary as output changes, per unit of output
The costs that do not vary as output changes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes average total cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cost of producing a typical unit of output
The cost of producing one more unit of output
The costs that vary as output changes, per unit of output
The costs that do not vary as output changes, per unit of output
The costs that do not vary as output changes

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below contains costs incurred by Fantabulous Enterprises, Inc., but some values are missing.

Quantity | Variable costs | Total Costs | Marginal costs | Average total costs
-|-|-|-|-
0 |          | $\$100$ |          |          
1 |          | $\$279$ | $\$179$ |  $\$279$
2 | $\$320$ | $\$420$ | $\$141$ | $\$210$
3 | $\$447$ | $\$547$ | $\$127$ | $\$182$
4 | $\$584$ |          | $\$137$ | $\$173$
5 | $\$755$ | $\$855$ |          | $\$171$
6 |          | $\$1084$|$\$229$ | $\$180$
7 | $\$1295$ | $\$1395$ | $\$311$ | $\$200$

**What are this firm’s total fixed costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Total fixed costs cannot be determined using this data.
Total fixed costs will vary based on the amount of output

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below contains costs incurred by Fantabulous Enterprises, Inc., but some values are missing. 


Quantity | Variable costs | Total Costs | Marginal costs | Average total costs
-|-|-|-|-
0 |          | $\$100$ |          |          
1 |          | $\$279$ | $\$179$ |  $\$279$
2 | $\$320$ | $\$420$ | $\$141$ | $\$210$
3 | $\$447$ | $\$547$ | $\$127$ | $\$182$
4 | $\$592$ |          | $\$137$ | $\$173$
5 | $\$755$ | $\$855$ |          | $\$171$
6 |          | $\$1084$|$\$229$ | $\$180$
7 | $\$1295$ | $\$1395$ | $\$311$ | $\$200$

**What is the total cost of producing 4 units?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are two ways you can find the total cost of producing 4 units based on this table. One way is to add fixed costs to the variable cost of producing 4 units, and one is to multiply average total cost by the number of units produced.
Fixed costs are missing from this table, but when a firm produces zero units, its only costs are fixed costs. Therefore, the total cost of producing zero units is a firm’s fixed costs.
The variable cost of producing 4 units is $\$592$ and fixed costs are $\$100$
The total cost of producing 4 units is $\$692$:

$\begin{align}\text{Total cost} (4) &= \text{Fixed cost} + \text{Variable Cost} (4)\\\\ 
& = \$100 + \$592\\\\
& = \$692\end{align}$


Alternately, you can multiply average total cost by quantity to find total cost:

$\begin{align} \text{Total cost} & = ATC \times Q\\\\
&=\$173 \times 4\\\\
&=\$692 \end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below contains costs incurred by Fantabulous Enterprises, Inc., but some values are missing. Assume that the quantity where average total cost is minimized is a whole number and included in this table.

Quantity | Variable costs | Total Costs | Marginal costs | Average total costs
-|-|-|-|-
0 |          | $\$100$ |          |          
1 |          | $\$279$ | $\$179$ |  $\$279$
2 | $\$320$ | $\$420$ | $\$141$ | $\$210$
3 | $\$447$ | $\$547$ | $\$127$ | $\$182$
4 | $\$584$ |          | $\$137$ | $\$173$
5 | $\$755$ | $\$855$ |          | $\$171$
6 |          | $\$1084$|$\$229$ | $\$180$
7 | $\$1295$ | $\$1395$ | $\$311$ | $\$200$


**What is this firm’s marginal cost of producing the fifth unit of output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Average total cost is minimized when 5 units are produced.
The average total cost of 5 units is $\$171$.
Average total cost is equal to marginal cost when average total cost is minimized.

The marginal cost of the 5th unit equals $\$171$ because average total cost is minimized when 5 units are produced, and average total cost equals marginal cost when average total cost is minimized.

choices.content: This cannot be determined based on the information given.

assessment_items.question_content:
A firm currently produces $100$ units of output and has fixed costs of $\$1000$, variable costs of $\$8000$, and the marginal cost of $\$100$ for the $100^{\text{th}}$ unit.

**What is the average total cost of producing $100$ units?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The table below contains information about a firm’s current production:

Variable | Value 
-|-
Quantity | $100$
Total costs | $\$18{,}000$
Variable costs | $\$11{,}000$

**What is the firm’s average fixed cost for producing $100$ units?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A firm currently produces $1000$ units of output and has total costs of $\$15{,}000$ and fixed costs of $\$3{,}000$. 

**What is the firm’s average variable costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
